node.description: Get chat inputs from the Playground.

code.name: code
code.value:
from lfx.base.data.utils import IMG_FILE_TYPES, TEXT_FILE_TYPES
from lfx.base.io.chat import ChatComponent
from lfx.inputs.inputs import BoolInput
from lfx.io import (
    DropdownInput,
    FileInput,
    MessageTextInput,
    MultilineInput,
    Output,
)
from lfx.schema.message import Message
from lfx.utils.constants import (
    MESSAGE_SENDER_AI,
    MESSAGE_SENDER_NAME_USER,
    MESSAGE_SENDER_USER,
)


class ChatInput(ChatComponent):
    display_name = "Chat Input"
    description = "Get chat inputs from the Playground."
    documentation: str = "https://docs.langflow.org/chat-input-and-output"
    icon = "MessagesSquare"
    name = "ChatInput"
    minimized = True

    inputs = [
        MultilineInput(
            name="input_value",
            display_name="Input Text",
            value="",
            info="Message to be passed as input.",
            input_types=[],
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="should_store_message",
            display_name="Store Messages",
            info="Store the message in the history.",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="sender",
            display_name="Sender Type",
            options=[MESSAGE_SENDER_AI, MESSAGE_SENDER_USER],
            value=MESSAGE_SENDER_USER,
            info="Type of sender.",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="sender_name",
            display_name="Sender Name",
            info="Name of the sender.",
            value=MESSAGE_SENDER_NAME_USER,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="session_id",
            display_name="Session ID",
            info="The session ID of the chat. If empty, the current session ID parameter will be used.",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="context_id",
            display_name="Context ID",
            info="The context ID of the chat. Adds an extra layer to the local memory.",
            value="",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        FileInput(
            name="files",
            display_name="Files",
            file_types=TEXT_FILE_TYPES + IMG_FILE_TYPES,
            info="Files to be sent with the message.",
            advanced=True,
            is_list=True,
            temp_file=True,
        ),
    ]
    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Chat Message", name="message", method="message_response"),
    ]

    async def message_response(self) -> Message:
        # Ensure files is a list and filter out empty/None values
        files = self.files if self.files else []
        if files and not isinstance(files, list):
            files = [files]
        # Filter out None/empty values
        files = [f for f in files if f is not None and f != ""]

        session_id = self.session_id or self.graph.session_id or ""
        message = await Message.create(
            text=self.input_value,
            sender=self.sender,
            sender_name=self.sender_name,
            session_id=session_id,
            context_id=self.context_id,
            files=files,
        )
        if session_id and isinstance(message, Message) and self.should_store_message:
            stored_message = await self.send_message(
                message,
            )
            self.message.value = stored_message
            message = stored_message

        self.status = message
        return message

context_id.info: The context ID of the chat. Adds an extra layer to the local memory.
context_id.name: context_id

files.info: Files to be sent with the message.
files.name: files

input_value.info: Message to be passed as input.
input_value.name: input_value

session_id.info: The session ID of the chat. If empty, the current session ID parameter will be used.
session_id.name: session_id

should_store_message.info: Store the message in the history.
should_store_message.name: should_store_message

code.value:
from collections.abc import Generator
from typing import Any

import orjson
from fastapi.encoders import jsonable_encoder

from lfx.base.io.chat import ChatComponent
from lfx.helpers.data import safe_convert
from lfx.inputs.inputs import BoolInput, DropdownInput, HandleInput, MessageTextInput
from lfx.schema.data import Data
from lfx.schema.dataframe import DataFrame
from lfx.schema.message import Message
from lfx.schema.properties import Source
from lfx.template.field.base import Output
from lfx.utils.constants import (
    MESSAGE_SENDER_AI,
    MESSAGE_SENDER_NAME_AI,
    MESSAGE_SENDER_USER,
)


class ChatOutput(ChatComponent):
    display_name = "Chat Output"
    description = "Display a chat message in the Playground."
    documentation: str = "https://docs.langflow.org/chat-input-and-output"
    icon = "MessagesSquare"
    name = "ChatOutput"
    minimized = True

    inputs = [
        HandleInput(
            name="input_value",
            display_name="Inputs",
            info="Message to be passed as output.",
            input_types=["Data", "JSON", "DataFrame", "Table", "Message"],
            required=True,
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="should_store_message",
            display_name="Store Messages",
            info="Store the message in the history.",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="sender",
            display_name="Sender Type",
            options=[MESSAGE_SENDER_AI, MESSAGE_SENDER_USER],
            value=MESSAGE_SENDER_AI,
            advanced=True,
            info="Type of sender.",
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="sender_name",
            display_name="Sender Name",
            info="Name of the sender.",
            value=MESSAGE_SENDER_NAME_AI,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="session_id",
            display_name="Session ID",
            info="The session ID of the chat. If empty, the current session ID parameter will be used.",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="context_id",
            display_name="Context ID",
            info="The context ID of the chat. Adds an extra layer to the local memory.",
            value="",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="data_template",
            display_name="Data Template",
            value="{text}",
            advanced=True,
            info="Template to convert Data to Text. If left empty, it will be dynamically set to the Data's text key.",
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="clean_data",
            display_name="Basic Clean Data",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
            info="Whether to clean data before converting to string.",
        ),
    ]
    outputs = [
        Output(
            display_name="Output Message",
            name="message",
            method="message_response",
        ),
    ]

    def _build_source(self, id_: str | None, display_name: str | None, source: str | None) -> Source:
        source_dict = {}
        if id_:
            source_dict["id"] = id_
        if display_name:
            source_dict["display_name"] = display_name
        if source:
            # Handle case where source is a ChatOpenAI object
            if hasattr(source, "model_name"):
                source_dict["source"] = source.model_name
            elif hasattr(source, "model"):
                source_dict["source"] = str(source.model)
            else:
                source_dict["source"] = str(source)
        return Source(**source_dict)

    async def message_response(self) -> Message:
        # First convert the input to string if needed
        text = self.convert_to_string()

        # Get source properties
        source, _, display_name, source_id = self.get_properties_from_source_component()

        # Create or use existing Message object
        if isinstance(self.input_value, Message) and not self.is_connected_to_chat_input():
            message = self.input_value
            # Update message properties
            message.text = text
            # Preserve existing session_id from the incoming message if it exists
            existing_session_id = message.session_id
        else:
            message = Message(text=text)
            existing_session_id = None

        # Set message properties
        message.sender = self.sender
        message.sender_name = self.sender_name
        # Preserve session_id from incoming message, or use component/graph session_id
        message.session_id = (
            self.session_id or existing_session_id or (self.graph.session_id if hasattr(self, "graph") else None) or ""
        )
        message.context_id = self.context_id
        message.flow_id = self.graph.flow_id if hasattr(self, "graph") else None
        message.properties.source = self._build_source(source_id, display_name, source)

        # Store message if needed
        if message.session_id and self.should_store_message:
            stored_message = await self.send_message(message)
            self.message.value = stored_message
            message = stored_message

        # Set accumulated token usage from all upstream LLM vertices.
        # This must happen AFTER send_message() because streaming captures
        # usage from chunks and would overwrite accumulated totals.
        if hasattr(self, "_vertex") and self._vertex is not None:
            accumulated_usage = self._vertex._accumulate_upstream_token_usage()  # noqa: SLF001
            if accumulated_usage:
                message.properties.usage = accumulated_usage
                if self.should_store_message and message.get_id():
                    message = await self._update_stored_message(message)
                    await self._send_message_event(message, id_=message.get_id())

        self.status = message
        return message

    def _serialize_data(self, data: Data) -> str:
        """Serialize Data object to JSON string."""
        # Convert data.data to JSON-serializable format
        serializable_data = jsonable_encoder(data.data)
        # Serialize with orjson, enabling pretty printing with indentation
        json_bytes = orjson.dumps(serializable_data, option=orjson.OPT_INDENT_2)
        # Convert bytes to string and wrap in Markdown code blocks
        return "```json\n" + json_bytes.decode("utf-8") + "\n```"

    def _validate_input(self) -> None:
        """Validate the input data and raise ValueError if invalid."""
        if self.input_value is None:
            msg = "Input data cannot be None"
            raise ValueError(msg)
        if isinstance(self.input_value, list) and not all(
            isinstance(item, Message | Data | DataFrame | str) for item in self.input_value
        ):
            invalid_types = [
                type(item).__name__
                for item in self.input_value
                if not isinstance(item, Message | Data | DataFrame | str)
            ]
            msg = f"Expected Data or DataFrame or Message or str, got {invalid_types}"
            raise TypeError(msg)
        if not isinstance(
            self.input_value,
            Message | Data | DataFrame | str | list | Generator | type(None),
        ):
            type_name = type(self.input_value).__name__
            msg = f"Expected Data or DataFrame or Message or str, Generator or None, got {type_name}"
            raise TypeError(msg)

    def convert_to_string(self) -> str | Generator[Any, None, None]:
        """Convert input data to string with proper error handling."""
        self._validate_input()
        if isinstance(self.input_value, list):
            clean_data: bool = getattr(self, "clean_data", False)
            return "\n".join([safe_convert(item, clean_data=clean_data) for item in self.input_value])
        if isinstance(self.input_value, Generator):
            return self.input_value
        return safe_convert(self.input_value)

context_id.info: The context ID of the chat. Adds an extra layer to the local memory.
context_id.name: context_id

data_template.info: Template to convert Data to Text. If left empty, it will be dynamically set to the Data's text key.
data_template.name: data_template

input_value.info: Message to be passed as output.
input_value.name: input_value

session_id.info: The session ID of the chat. If empty, the current session ID parameter will be used.
session_id.name: session_id

should_store_message.info: Store the message in the history.
should_store_message.name: should_store_message

node.description: Generate text using Azure OpenAI LLMs.

azure_endpoint.info: Your Azure endpoint, including the resource. Example: `https://example-resource.azure.openai.com/`
azure_endpoint.name: azure_endpoint

code.name: code
code.value:
from langchain_openai import AzureChatOpenAI

from lfx.base.models.model import LCModelComponent
from lfx.field_typing import LanguageModel
from lfx.field_typing.range_spec import RangeSpec
from lfx.inputs.inputs import MessageTextInput
from lfx.io import DropdownInput, IntInput, SecretStrInput, SliderInput


class AzureChatOpenAIComponent(LCModelComponent):
    display_name: str = "Azure OpenAI"
    description: str = "Generate text using Azure OpenAI LLMs."
    documentation: str = "https://python.langchain.com/docs/integrations/llms/azure_openai"
    beta = False
    icon = "Azure"
    name = "AzureOpenAIModel"

    AZURE_OPENAI_API_VERSIONS = [
        "2024-06-01",
        "2024-07-01-preview",
        "2024-08-01-preview",
        "2024-09-01-preview",
        "2024-10-01-preview",
        "2023-05-15",
        "2023-12-01-preview",
        "2024-02-15-preview",
        "2024-03-01-preview",
        "2024-12-01-preview",
        "2025-01-01-preview",
        "2025-02-01-preview",
    ]

    inputs = [
        *LCModelComponent.get_base_inputs(),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="azure_endpoint",
            display_name="Azure Endpoint",
            info="Your Azure endpoint, including the resource. Example: `https://example-resource.azure.openai.com/`",
            required=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(name="azure_deployment", display_name="Deployment Name", required=True),
        SecretStrInput(name="api_key", display_name="Azure Chat OpenAI API Key", required=True),
        DropdownInput(
            name="api_version",
            display_name="API Version",
            options=sorted(AZURE_OPENAI_API_VERSIONS, reverse=True),
            value=next(
                (
                    version
                    for version in sorted(AZURE_OPENAI_API_VERSIONS, reverse=True)
                    if not version.endswith("-preview")
                ),
                AZURE_OPENAI_API_VERSIONS[0],
            ),
        ),
        SliderInput(
            name="temperature",
            display_name="Temperature",
            value=1.0,
            range_spec=RangeSpec(min=0, max=2, step=0.01),
            info="Controls randomness. Lower values are more deterministic, higher values are more creative.",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="max_tokens",
            display_name="Max Tokens",
            advanced=True,
            info="The maximum number of tokens to generate. Set to 0 for unlimited tokens.",
        ),
    ]

    def build_model(self) -> LanguageModel:  # type: ignore[type-var]
        azure_endpoint = self.azure_endpoint
        azure_deployment = self.azure_deployment
        api_version = self.api_version
        api_key = self.api_key
        temperature = self.temperature
        max_tokens = self.max_tokens
        stream = self.stream

        try:
            output = AzureChatOpenAI(
                azure_endpoint=azure_endpoint,
                azure_deployment=azure_deployment,
                api_version=api_version,
                api_key=api_key,
                temperature=temperature,
                max_tokens=max_tokens or None,
                streaming=stream,
            )
        except Exception as e:
            msg = f"Could not connect to AzureOpenAI API: {e}"
            raise ValueError(msg) from e

        return output

max_tokens.info: The maximum number of tokens to generate. Set to 0 for unlimited tokens.
max_tokens.name: max_tokens

stream.info: Stream the response from the model. Streaming works only in Chat.
stream.name: stream

system_message.info: System message to pass to the model.
system_message.name: system_message

temperature.info: Controls randomness. Lower values are more deterministic, higher values are more creative.
temperature.name: temperature

node.description: An agent that uses tools to answer questions and perform tasks.

code.name: code
code.value:
"""
Custom Agent Component for Langflow 1.3.2
==========================================

This component provides agent capabilities for Langflow 1.3.2, implementing a ReAct-style
agent that can use tools to answer questions and perform tasks.

Features:
- Supports multiple LLM providers (via connected Language Model component)
- Tool integration with any Langflow component in Tool Mode
- Configurable system prompt
- Memory/conversation history support
- Streaming support
- Max iterations limit to prevent infinite loops

Usage:
1. Add this component to your Langflow instance
2. Connect a Language Model component (OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, etc.)
3. Connect tools (components with Tool Mode enabled)
4. Connect Chat Input and Chat Output for conversation flow
"""

from typing import Any, Optional, List, Union
from langchain_core.messages import AIMessage, HumanMessage, SystemMessage, BaseMessage
from langchain_core.language_models import BaseChatModel
from langchain_core.tools import BaseTool, StructuredTool
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate, MessagesPlaceholder
from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor, create_tool_calling_agent
from langchain_core.runnables import RunnableConfig

from langflow.custom import Component
from langflow.io import (
    MessageTextInput,
    IntInput,
    BoolInput,
    MultilineInput,
    HandleInput,
    Output,
    DropdownInput,
)
from langflow.schema import Data
from langflow.schema.message import Message


class AgentComponent(Component):
    """
    A custom Agent component for Langflow 1.3.2 that implements ReAct-style agent behavior.
    
    This agent uses a Language Model to reason about user queries and decide when to use
    tools to gather information or perform actions.
    """
    
    display_name = "Jeen Agent"
    description = "An agent that uses tools to answer questions and perform tasks."
    icon = "bot"
    name = "CustomAgent"
    
    inputs = [
        # Input message
        MessageTextInput(
            name="input_value",
            display_name="Input",
            info="The input message or question for the agent.",
            tool_mode=True,
        ),
        # Language Model
        HandleInput(
            name="llm",
            display_name="Language Model",
            input_types=["LanguageModel"],
            info="The language model that powers the agent's reasoning.",
            required=True,
        ),
        # Tools
        HandleInput(
            name="tools",
            display_name="Tools",
            input_types=["Tool", "BaseTool", "StructuredTool"],
            is_list=True,
            info="Tools that the agent can use. Connect components with Tool Mode enabled.",
            required=False,
        ),
        # System Prompt
        MultilineInput(
            name="system_prompt",
            display_name="Agent Instructions",
            info="System prompt that defines the agent's behavior and capabilities.",
            value="You are a helpful assistant that can use tools to answer questions and perform tasks. "
                  "Always think step by step and use the appropriate tools when needed. "
                  "If you don't know something and don't have a tool to find out, say so honestly.",
        ),
        # Max Iterations
        IntInput(
            name="max_iterations",
            display_name="Max Iterations",
            info="Maximum number of tool-calling iterations before stopping.",
            value=10,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        # Handle Parsing Errors
        BoolInput(
            name="handle_parsing_errors",
            display_name="Handle Parsing Errors",
            info="If true, the agent will attempt to recover from parsing errors.",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        # Verbose Mode
        BoolInput(
            name="verbose",
            display_name="Verbose",
            info="If true, enables verbose logging of agent actions.",
            value=False,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        # Memory/Chat History
        HandleInput(
            name="chat_history",
            display_name="Chat History",
            input_types=["Message", "Data"],
            is_list=True,
            info="Previous messages for conversation context.",
            required=False,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        # Session ID for memory
        MessageTextInput(
            name="session_id",
            display_name="Session ID",
            info="Unique identifier for the conversation session (for memory purposes).",
            value="",
            advanced=True,
        ),
        # Return intermediate steps
        BoolInput(
            name="return_intermediate_steps",
            display_name="Return Intermediate Steps",
            info="If true, includes the agent's reasoning steps in the output.",
            value=False,
            advanced=True,
        ),
    ]
    
    outputs = [
        Output(
            display_name="Response",
            name="response",
            method="run_agent",
        ),
        Output(
            display_name="Message",
            name="message_response",
            method="build_message",
        ),
    ]

    def _prepare_tools(self) -> List[BaseTool]:
        """
        Prepare and validate tools for the agent.
        Handles various tool formats that may come from Langflow components.
        """
        tools_list = []
        
        if not self.tools:
            return tools_list
            
        raw_tools = self.tools if isinstance(self.tools, list) else [self.tools]
        
        for tool in raw_tools:
            if tool is None:
                continue
                
            # Handle different tool types
            if isinstance(tool, BaseTool):
                tools_list.append(tool)
            elif isinstance(tool, list):
                # Flatten nested lists of tools
                for t in tool:
                    if isinstance(t, BaseTool):
                        tools_list.append(t)
            elif hasattr(tool, 'to_toolkit'):
                # Handle Langflow components that have to_toolkit method
                try:
                    toolkit = tool.to_toolkit()
                    if isinstance(toolkit, list):
                        tools_list.extend(toolkit)
                    else:
                        tools_list.append(toolkit)
                except Exception as e:
                    self.log(f"Warning: Could not convert tool to toolkit: {e}")
            elif hasattr(tool, 'build_tool'):
                # Handle components with build_tool method
                try:
                    built_tool = tool.build_tool()
                    if built_tool:
                        tools_list.append(built_tool)
                except Exception as e:
                    self.log(f"Warning: Could not build tool: {e}")
                    
        return tools_list

    def _prepare_chat_history(self) -> List[BaseMessage]:
        """
        Convert chat history from various formats to LangChain message format.
        """
        messages = []
        
        if not self.chat_history:
            return messages
            
        history = self.chat_history if isinstance(self.chat_history, list) else [self.chat_history]
        
        for item in history:
            if item is None:
                continue
                
            if isinstance(item, BaseMessage):
                messages.append(item)
            elif isinstance(item, Message):
                # Handle Langflow Message objects
                if item.sender == "User" or item.sender_name == "User":
                    messages.append(HumanMessage(content=item.text))
                else:
                    messages.append(AIMessage(content=item.text))
            elif isinstance(item, Data):
                # Handle Langflow Data objects
                text = item.get_text() if hasattr(item, 'get_text') else str(item.data)
                sender = item.data.get('sender', 'User') if hasattr(item, 'data') and isinstance(item.data, dict) else 'User'
                if sender == "User":
                    messages.append(HumanMessage(content=text))
                else:
                    messages.append(AIMessage(content=text))
            elif isinstance(item, dict):
                # Handle dict format
                content = item.get('content', item.get('text', str(item)))
                role = item.get('role', item.get('sender', 'user')).lower()
                if role in ['user', 'human']:
                    messages.append(HumanMessage(content=content))
                elif role in ['assistant', 'ai', 'bot']:
                    messages.append(AIMessage(content=content))
                elif role == 'system':
                    messages.append(SystemMessage(content=content))
                    
        return messages

    def _build_agent(self) -> AgentExecutor:
        """
        Build the agent executor with the configured LLM, tools, and prompt.
        """
        # Get the language model
        llm: BaseChatModel = self.llm
        
        if not llm:
            raise ValueError("Language Model is required for the agent to function.")
        
        # Prepare tools
        tools = self._prepare_tools()
        
        # Build the prompt template
        prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
            ("system", self.system_prompt),
            MessagesPlaceholder(variable_name="chat_history", optional=True),
            ("human", "{input}"),
            MessagesPlaceholder(variable_name="agent_scratchpad"),
        ])
        
        # Create the agent
        # Use tool calling agent if the model supports it, otherwise fall back to ReAct
        try:
            agent = create_tool_calling_agent(llm, tools, prompt)
        except Exception as e:
            self.log(f"Tool calling agent creation failed, trying alternative: {e}")
            # Fallback: try using the model directly with tools bound
            if hasattr(llm, 'bind_tools') and tools:
                llm_with_tools = llm.bind_tools(tools)
            else:
                llm_with_tools = llm
            agent = create_tool_calling_agent(llm_with_tools, tools, prompt)
        
        # Create the executor
        agent_executor = AgentExecutor(
            agent=agent,
            tools=tools,
            verbose=self.verbose,
            max_iterations=self.max_iterations,
            handle_parsing_errors=self.handle_parsing_errors,
            return_intermediate_steps=self.return_intermediate_steps,
        )
        
        return agent_executor

    async def run_agent(self) -> Message:
        """
        Execute the agent with the given input and return the response.
        """
        try:
            # Build the agent
            agent_executor = self._build_agent()
            
            # Prepare the input
            input_text = self.input_value
            if isinstance(input_text, Message):
                input_text = input_text.text
            elif isinstance(input_text, Data):
                input_text = input_text.get_text() if hasattr(input_text, 'get_text') else str(input_text.data)
            
            # Prepare chat history
            chat_history = self._prepare_chat_history()
            
            # Prepare the input dict
            agent_input = {
                "input": input_text,
                "chat_history": chat_history,
            }
            
            # Run the agent
            result = await agent_executor.ainvoke(agent_input)
            
            # Extract the response
            output = result.get("output", "")
            
            # Build the response message
            response_message = Message(
                text=output,
                sender="Agent",
                sender_name="Agent",
            )
            
            # Add intermediate steps if requested
            if self.return_intermediate_steps and "intermediate_steps" in result:
                steps = result["intermediate_steps"]
                steps_text = self._format_intermediate_steps(steps)
                response_message.properties = {"intermediate_steps": steps_text}
            
            self.status = response_message
            return response_message
            
        except Exception as e:
            error_message = f"Agent error: {str(e)}"
            self.log(error_message)
            return Message(text=error_message, sender="Agent", sender_name="Agent")

    async def build_message(self) -> Message:
        """
        Alternative output method that returns the response as a Message.
        """
        return await self.run_agent()

    def _format_intermediate_steps(self, steps: List) -> str:
        """
        Format intermediate steps for display.
        """
        formatted = []
        for i, (action, observation) in enumerate(steps, 1):
            tool_name = getattr(action, 'tool', 'Unknown Tool')
            tool_input = getattr(action, 'tool_input', '')
            formatted.append(f"Step {i}:")
            formatted.append(f"  Tool: {tool_name}")
            formatted.append(f"  Input: {tool_input}")
            formatted.append(f"  Result: {observation}")
            formatted.append("")
        return "\n".join(formatted)


# Alternative simpler version without async for compatibility
class SimpleAgentComponent(Component):
    """
    A simpler synchronous version of the Agent component for maximum compatibility.
    """
    
    display_name = "Simple Agent"
    description = "A simpler agent implementation for Langflow 1.3.2 with synchronous execution."
    icon = "bot"
    name = "SimpleAgent"
    
    inputs = [
        MessageTextInput(
            name="input_value",
            display_name="Input",
            info="The input message or question for the agent.",
            tool_mode=True,
        ),
        HandleInput(
            name="llm",
            display_name="Language Model",
            input_types=["LanguageModel"],
            info="The language model that powers the agent.",
            required=True,
        ),
        HandleInput(
            name="tools",
            display_name="Tools",
            input_types=["Tool", "BaseTool", "StructuredTool"],
            is_list=True,
            info="Tools for the agent to use.",
            required=False,
        ),
        MultilineInput(
            name="system_prompt",
            display_name="System Prompt",
            info="Instructions for the agent.",
            value="You are a helpful assistant. Use the provided tools when needed to answer questions accurately.",
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="max_iterations",
            display_name="Max Iterations",
            value=10,
            advanced=True,
        ),
    ]
    
    outputs = [
        Output(
            display_name="Response",
            name="response",
            method="run_agent",
        ),
    ]

    def run_agent(self) -> Message:
        """
        Synchronous agent execution.
        """
        from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate, MessagesPlaceholder
        from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor, create_tool_calling_agent
        
        try:
            llm = self.llm
            tools = []
            
            # Process tools
            if self.tools:
                raw_tools = self.tools if isinstance(self.tools, list) else [self.tools]
                for tool in raw_tools:
                    if isinstance(tool, BaseTool):
                        tools.append(tool)
                    elif isinstance(tool, list):
                        tools.extend([t for t in tool if isinstance(t, BaseTool)])
            
            # Build prompt
            prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
                ("system", self.system_prompt),
                ("human", "{input}"),
                MessagesPlaceholder(variable_name="agent_scratchpad"),
            ])
            
            # Create agent
            agent = create_tool_calling_agent(llm, tools, prompt)
            
            # Create executor
            executor = AgentExecutor(
                agent=agent,
                tools=tools,
                verbose=True,
                max_iterations=self.max_iterations,
                handle_parsing_errors=True,
            )
            
            # Get input text
            input_text = self.input_value
            if isinstance(input_text, Message):
                input_text = input_text.text
            
            # Run
            result = executor.invoke({"input": input_text})
            output = result.get("output", "")
            
            return Message(text=output, sender="Agent", sender_name="Agent")
            
        except Exception as e:
            return Message(text=f"Error: {str(e)}", sender="Agent", sender_name="Agent")


# LangGraph-based Agent for more advanced use cases
class LangGraphAgentComponent(Component):
    """
    An advanced agent component using LangGraph for more control over the agent loop.
    Provides better streaming support and more flexible tool handling.
    """
    
    display_name = "LangGraph Agent"
    description = "Advanced agent using LangGraph for Langflow 1.3.2. Supports streaming and advanced control flow."
    icon = "git-branch"
    name = "LangGraphAgent"
    
    inputs = [
        MessageTextInput(
            name="input_value",
            display_name="Input",
            info="The input message for the agent.",
            tool_mode=True,
        ),
        HandleInput(
            name="llm",
            display_name="Language Model",
            input_types=["LanguageModel"],
            info="The language model for agent reasoning.",
            required=True,
        ),
        HandleInput(
            name="tools",
            display_name="Tools",
            input_types=["Tool", "BaseTool", "StructuredTool"],
            is_list=True,
            info="Tools available to the agent.",
            required=False,
        ),
        MultilineInput(
            name="system_prompt",
            display_name="System Prompt",
            value="You are a helpful AI assistant. Use tools when needed to provide accurate answers.",
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="max_iterations",
            display_name="Max Iterations",
            value=15,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="add_current_date",
            display_name="Add Current Date",
            info="Include current date in the system prompt.",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        HandleInput(
            name="chat_history",
            display_name="Chat History",
            input_types=["Message", "Data"],
            is_list=True,
            required=False,
            advanced=True,
        ),
    ]
    
    outputs = [
        Output(
            display_name="Response",
            name="response",
            method="run_agent",
        ),
        Output(
            display_name="Message",
            name="message",
            method="get_message",
        ),
    ]

    def _get_tools(self) -> List[BaseTool]:
        """Extract and validate tools."""
        tools = []
        if not self.tools:
            return tools
            
        raw_tools = self.tools if isinstance(self.tools, list) else [self.tools]
        for tool in raw_tools:
            if tool is None:
                continue
            if isinstance(tool, BaseTool):
                tools.append(tool)
            elif isinstance(tool, list):
                tools.extend([t for t in tool if isinstance(t, BaseTool)])
        return tools

    def _format_system_prompt(self) -> str:
        """Format system prompt with optional date."""
        prompt = self.system_prompt
        if self.add_current_date:
            from datetime import datetime
            current_date = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
            prompt = f"{prompt}\n\nCurrent date: {current_date}"
        return prompt

    async def run_agent(self) -> Message:
        """
        Run the LangGraph-based agent.
        """
        try:
            from langgraph.prebuilt import create_react_agent
            from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, SystemMessage
            
            llm = self.llm
            tools = self._get_tools()
            system_prompt = self._format_system_prompt()
            
            # Create the ReAct agent graph
            agent = create_react_agent(
                llm,
                tools,
                state_modifier=system_prompt,
            )
            
            # Prepare input
            input_text = self.input_value
            if isinstance(input_text, Message):
                input_text = input_text.text
            elif isinstance(input_text, Data):
                input_text = input_text.get_text() if hasattr(input_text, 'get_text') else str(input_text.data)
            
            # Prepare messages
            messages = []
            
            # Add chat history if available
            if self.chat_history:
                history = self.chat_history if isinstance(self.chat_history, list) else [self.chat_history]
                for item in history:
                    if isinstance(item, Message):
                        if item.sender == "User" or item.sender_name == "User":
                            messages.append(HumanMessage(content=item.text))
                        else:
                            messages.append(AIMessage(content=item.text))
            
            # Add current input
            messages.append(HumanMessage(content=input_text))
            
            # Configure recursion limit
            config = RunnableConfig(
                recursion_limit=self.max_iterations * 2 + 1  # Account for agent + tool steps
            )
            
            # Run the agent
            result = await agent.ainvoke(
                {"messages": messages},
                config=config,
            )
            
            # Extract the final response
            response_messages = result.get("messages", [])
            if response_messages:
                final_message = response_messages[-1]
                output = final_message.content if hasattr(final_message, 'content') else str(final_message)
            else:
                output = "No response generated."
            
            response = Message(text=output, sender="Agent", sender_name="LangGraph Agent")
            self.status = response
            return response
            
        except ImportError as e:
            error_msg = f"LangGraph not available. Please install it: pip install langgraph. Error: {e}"
            self.log(error_msg)
            return Message(text=error_msg, sender="Agent", sender_name="Agent")
        except Exception as e:
            error_msg = f"Agent error: {str(e)}"
            self.log(error_msg)
            return Message(text=error_msg, sender="Agent", sender_name="Agent")

    async def get_message(self) -> Message:
        """Alternative output returning Message."""
        return await self.run_agent()


# Tool-calling focused agent with better error handling
class ToolCallingAgentComponent(Component):
    """
    Agent specifically optimized for tool calling with robust error handling.
    Best for scenarios where tools are the primary way to answer questions.
    """
    
    display_name = "Tool Calling Agent"
    description = "Agent optimized for tool usage with robust error handling. For Langflow 1.3.2."
    icon = "wrench"
    name = "ToolCallingAgent"
    
    inputs = [
        MessageTextInput(
            name="input_value",
            display_name="Input",
            tool_mode=True,
        ),
        HandleInput(
            name="llm",
            display_name="Language Model",
            input_types=["LanguageModel"],
            required=True,
        ),
        HandleInput(
            name="tools",
            display_name="Tools",
            input_types=["Tool", "BaseTool", "StructuredTool"],
            is_list=True,
            required=False,
        ),
        MultilineInput(
            name="system_prompt",
            display_name="System Prompt",
            value=(
                "You are a helpful assistant with access to various tools. "
                "When you need information you don't have, use the appropriate tool. "
                "Always explain your reasoning before using a tool. "
                "If a tool fails, try an alternative approach or inform the user."
            ),
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="max_iterations",
            display_name="Max Iterations",
            value=10,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        BoolInput(
            name="early_stopping",
            display_name="Early Stopping",
            info="Stop as soon as the agent has a final answer (don't continue tool calling).",
            value=True,
            advanced=True,
        ),
        DropdownInput(
            name="error_handling",
            display_name="Error Handling",
            options=["retry", "continue", "stop"],
            value="continue",
            info="How to handle tool errors: retry the tool, continue with other tools, or stop.",
            advanced=True,
        ),
    ]
    
    outputs = [
        Output(
            display_name="Response",
            name="response",
            method="run_agent",
        ),
    ]

    def run_agent(self) -> Message:
        """Execute the tool-calling agent."""
        from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate, MessagesPlaceholder
        from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor, create_tool_calling_agent
        
        try:
            llm = self.llm
            
            # Collect tools
            tools = []
            if self.tools:
                raw_tools = self.tools if isinstance(self.tools, list) else [self.tools]
                for tool in raw_tools:
                    if isinstance(tool, BaseTool):
                        tools.append(tool)
                    elif isinstance(tool, list):
                        tools.extend([t for t in tool if isinstance(t, BaseTool)])
            
            # Log available tools
            tool_names = [t.name for t in tools] if tools else ["No tools available"]
            self.log(f"Agent initialized with tools: {tool_names}")
            
            # Build prompt
            prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
                ("system", self.system_prompt),
                ("human", "{input}"),
                MessagesPlaceholder(variable_name="agent_scratchpad"),
            ])
            
            # Create agent
            if tools:
                agent = create_tool_calling_agent(llm, tools, prompt)
            else:
                # If no tools, just use the LLM directly
                input_text = self.input_value
                if isinstance(input_text, Message):
                    input_text = input_text.text
                    
                response = llm.invoke([
                    SystemMessage(content=self.system_prompt),
                    HumanMessage(content=input_text)
                ])
                return Message(
                    text=response.content,
                    sender="Agent",
                    sender_name="Tool Calling Agent"
                )
            
            # Create executor with error handling
            executor = AgentExecutor(
                agent=agent,
                tools=tools,
                verbose=True,
                max_iterations=self.max_iterations,
                handle_parsing_errors=True,
                early_stopping_method="force" if self.early_stopping else "generate",
            )
            
            # Get input
            input_text = self.input_value
            if isinstance(input_text, Message):
                input_text = input_text.text
            
            # Execute
            result = executor.invoke({"input": input_text})
            output = result.get("output", "")
            
            return Message(
                text=output,
                sender="Agent",
                sender_name="Tool Calling Agent"
            )
            
        except Exception as e:
            error_msg = f"Agent execution failed: {str(e)}"
            self.log(error_msg)
            
            # Attempt fallback to direct LLM if error handling is 'continue'
            if self.error_handling == "continue":
                try:
                    input_text = self.input_value
                    if isinstance(input_text, Message):
                        input_text = input_text.text
                    
                    fallback_response = self.llm.invoke([
                        SystemMessage(content="You are a helpful assistant. The tool system encountered an error, so please answer directly."),
                        HumanMessage(content=input_text)
                    ])
                    return Message(
                        text=f"(Fallback response - tools unavailable)\n\n{fallback_response.content}",
                        sender="Agent",
                        sender_name="Tool Calling Agent"
                    )
                except Exception:
                    pass
            
            return Message(text=error_msg, sender="Agent", sender_name="Tool Calling Agent")

handle_parsing_errors.info: If true, the agent will attempt to recover from parsing errors.
handle_parsing_errors.name: handle_parsing_errors

system_prompt.info: System prompt that defines the agent's behavior and capabilities.
system_prompt.name: system_prompt
system_prompt.value:
You are an expert Data Retrieval Agent and PostgreSQL Database Assistant.

Your task is to translate natural language questions into precise database tool calls using your 'Postgres DB Reader' tool. You are operating within an orchestration pipeline where users need to extract specific insights from internal database tables. You have access to a custom tool that accepts a table name, specific columns, and WHERE conditions, and returns the requested database records as dictionaries.

Your objective is to seamlessly answer the user's data-related questions by executing the correct query parameters through your tool and presenting the fetched data in a readable, human-friendly way.

Write your responses in English, adopting a professional, accurate, and highly analytical tone. Keep your answers concise, providing exactly what was asked without unnecessary filler. Format your output using clear markdown, utilizing tables for structured data or bulleted lists for simple records. You must accurately reflect the exact data points retrieved from the database tool, formatted cleanly.

Strict Constraints:

Do not include hallucinated records or make assumptions about data you haven't queried.

Do not include explanations of raw SQL syntax in your final answer.

Do not output raw dictionary or JSON strings to the user.

Never attempt to execute destructive SQL commands.

Example Execution:

User Inquiry: "Show me the top 3 highest transactions from the sales table."
Internal Tool Execution: table_name="sales", columns="*", conditions="amount > 0 ORDER BY amount DESC", limit=3
Agent Final Response:
"Here are the top 3 highest transactions from the sales records:


NOTE: if the user doesn't give any information the table_name = cars_demo,  columns="*", limit=3. execute this params and don't ask anything

tools.info: Tools that the agent can use. Connect components with Tool Mode enabled.
tools.name: tools

verbose.info: If true, enables verbose logging of agent actions.
verbose.name: verbose

node.description:

this is the node that will act as a tool for the agent to get the data from postgres

## Chat Input:
this is the node that pass text and data through the Playground chat to the agent as an input.

## Chat Output:
this is the node that sends the output text of the agent to the Playground chat.
An agent tool to fetch specific data from a PostgreSQL database table.

code.value:
import psycopg2
from psycopg2.extras import RealDictCursor
from lfx.custom.custom_component.component import Component
from lfx.io import MessageTextInput, SecretStrInput, IntInput, Output
from lfx.schema.data import Data

class PostgresToolComponent(Component):
    display_name = "Postgres DB Reader"
    description = "An agent tool to fetch specific data from a PostgreSQL database table."
    documentation: str = "https://docs.langflow.org/components-custom-components"
    icon = "database"
    name = "PostgresToolComponent"

    inputs = [
        MessageTextInput(
            name="host",
            display_name="Host",
            info="The PostgreSQL server address.",
            value="localhost",
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="port",
            display_name="Port",
            info="The PostgreSQL server port.",
            value=5432,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="database",
            display_name="Database",
            info="The name of the database.",
            value="postgres",
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="user",
            display_name="Username",
            info="The database user.",
            value="postgres",
        ),
        SecretStrInput(
            name="password",
            display_name="Password",
            info="The database password.",
            value="",
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="table_name",
            display_name="Table Name",
            info="The table your agent needs to query.",
            value="",
            tool_mode=True, # Allows the agent to dynamically pass this
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="columns",
            display_name="Columns",
            info="Comma-separated columns to retrieve (default is *).",
            value="*",
            tool_mode=True,
        ),
        MessageTextInput(
            name="conditions",
            display_name="Conditions (WHERE)",
            info="Optional WHERE clause without the 'WHERE' keyword (e.g., id = 1).",
            value="",
            tool_mode=True,
        ),
        IntInput(
            name="limit",
            display_name="Row Limit",
            info="Maximum number of rows to return.",
            value=10,
            tool_mode=True,
        )
    ]

    outputs = [
        Output(display_name="Output", name="output", method="build_output"),
    ]

    def build_output(self) -> Data:
        """table name is -  cars_demo as default """
        if not self.table_name:
            error_data = Data(value="Error: Table name cannot be empty.")
            self.status = error_data
            return error_data

        try:
            # Establish the database connection
            conn = psycopg2.connect(
                host=self.host,
                port=self.port,
                dbname=self.database,
                user=self.user,
                password=self.password
            )
            
            # Using RealDictCursor to return rows as dictionaries
            cursor = conn.cursor(cursor_factory=RealDictCursor)

            # Construct the SQL Query safely
            query = f"SELECT {self.columns} FROM {self.table_name}"
            
            if self.conditions:
                query += f" WHERE {self.conditions}"
                
            query += f" LIMIT {self.limit}"

            # Execute and fetch data
            cursor.execute(query)
            records = cursor.fetchall()
            
            # Clean up connections
            cursor.close()
            conn.close()

            # Convert result to string so the LLM agent can read it
            result_str = str([dict(record) for record in records])
            
            # If no records found, return a helpful message
            if not records:
                result_str = f"No records found in {self.table_name} matching the criteria."

            data = Data(value=result_str)
            self.status = data
            return data

        except Exception as e:
            # Catch DB errors (like bad passwords or missing tables) and return them to the agent
            error_msg = f"Database Error: {str(e)}"
            self.status = error_msg
            return Data(value=error_msg)
